name: Commercial Lenders of America
description_short: Commercial Lenders of America offers commercial real estate financing, hard money loans, SBA loans, and working capital solutions for small to large businesses with up to $100M in loan amounts.
description_long:
Commercial Lenders of America positions itself as a commercial finance provider specializing in real estate investment and business lending. According to their website, they claim 30 years of experience in commercial loans and market themselves as a hard money/private money lender with capability to close quickly on deals conventional lenders reject.

Their service offerings span multiple loan categories: commercial real estate financing and refinancing, SBA loans, fix-and-flip loans, working capital loans (unsecured, no collateral required for amounts under $725,000), hard money loans, apartment/multifamily loans (up to 85% LTV), bridge loans, equipment financing, and accounts receivable financing. They market loan amounts up to $100 million and claim a simplified three-step process with potential funding within 72 hours. They serve diverse property types including multifamily residential, retail, hotels, healthcare facilities, industrial buildings, and special-use properties.

The company emphasizes fast processing and claims no upfront fees, distinguishing themselves through quick turnaround and willingness to work with borrowers conventional lenders have rejected. Their marketing focuses on speed, accessibility, and competitive pricing relative to conventional lenders, particularly for real estate investors and small business owners needing flexible financing.

Key limitation: The website provides marketing claims but limited independent verification of credentials, licensing, or regulatory standing. No information about actual rates, terms, company ownership structure, or specific regulatory compliance is disclosed. The breadth of products claimed (SBA loans to hard money to equipment financing) across such varied markets warrants due diligence verification before application.
pros: Claims no upfront fees for loan processing
Offers working capital loans as unsecured debt with no collateral required for amounts under $725,000
Advertises potential funding within 72 hours or less
Provides hard money loans up to 75% LTV for property purchases
Multifamily apartment financing up to 85% property value
Serves diverse borrower profiles and property types (multifamily, commercial, industrial, special-use)
Claims willingness to approve borrowers conventional lenders reject
cons: Website lacks specific rate, term, or APR information for any loan product
No disclosure of licensing, regulatory compliance, or NMLS registration visible
Hard money loans explicitly described as expensive and short-term, high-cost solutions
Claims of 30-year experience and company credentials not independently verifiable from website alone
No client testimonials, reviews, or third-party verification provided on website
best_for: Real estate investors seeking short-term fix-and-flip or bridge financing
Small business owners needing working capital without collateral for amounts under $725,000
Commercial property buyers rejected by traditional banks or seeking faster closing timelines
Multifamily property investors needing financing at 85% LTV or higher
diagnosis: Best for commercial real estate investors, small business owners, and borrowers needing fast capital when traditional banks decline or move too slowly. Primary caveat: hard money and bridge products are explicitly expensive short-term solutions; verify actual rates, terms, and regulatory standing before committing, as the website lacks specific pricing and compliance disclosures.
services: Commercial real estate financing and refinancing
SBA loans for small business owners
Fix-and-flip loans for investment property purchase and rehab
Working capital loans (unsecured, no collateral under $725,000)
Apartment and multifamily residential financing (up to 85% LTV)
meta_description: Commercial Lenders of America offers commercial real estate, hard money, and SBA loans for small to large businesses.
